title: EXAMPLE 3.15 Zero-Input Response of a Second-Order System with Repeated Roots; Complex Roots
summary: This page presents Example 3.15, deriving the zero-input response of a second-order discrete-time system with repeated characteristic roots and using MATLAB to solve for the constants from initial conditions. It then begins the discussion of complex roots, expressing conjugate roots in polar form and setting up the real-valued zero-input response representation.
